meta.name: PAGA trajectory (raw counts)
meta.description: Cluster raw counts, then abstract the cluster connectivity graph with PAGA (partition-based graph abstraction) to reveal candidate trajectories between clusters. Connectivities land in uns['paga']. For unprocessed data such as Xenium.
meta.provenance: scanpy PAGA workflow (sc.tl.paga; Wolf et al. 2019, scanpy trajectory-inference tutorial). Targets unprocessed raw-count data such as Xenium.

readme: Scanpy path from raw counts to PAGA: normalize -> log1p -> PCA -> neighbors -> Leiden -> PAGA -> UMAP (plus a 3-component UMAP in `obsm['X_umap_3d']` for the 3D scatter view). PAGA scores how strongly each pair of clusters is connected (uns['paga']['connectivities']); `cluster_key` names the obs column the clustering writes and PAGA groups by. UMAP uses its default (spectral) initialization — the PAGA-initialized layout needs sc.pl.paga, which this app does not expose.

params.name: cluster_key
params.tooltip: obs column the Leiden clustering writes and PAGA groups by

params.name: resolution
params.tooltip: Leiden resolution: higher = more, smaller clusters
